title: Step 4: Run the command to query your collection.

sections.heading: Prerequisites
sections.body:
Before you begin, complete the [common prerequisites](avs-tutorials-prereqs).
This tutorial includes examples for the following clients:

- [Atlas UI](fts-tutorial-run-query)

- [mongosh](mongodb-shell)

- [MongoDB C# Driver](csharp)

- [MongoDB Go Driver](go)

- [MongoDB Java Driver](java-drivers)

- [MongoDB Node Driver](node)

- [MongoDB Python Driver (PyMongo)](pymongo)

sections.heading: Create the MongoDB Vector Search Index
sections.body:
This section demonstrates how to create a MongoDB Vector Search index on the
`plot_embedding_voyage_3_large` field in the  `sample_mflix.embedded_movies`
collection for running vector queries against the field.

sections.heading: Required Access
sections.body: To create a MongoDB Vector Search index, you must have [Project Data Access Admin](<Project Data Access Admin>) or higher access to the project.

sections.heading: Procedure

codeBlocks.content:
New search index named vector_index is building.
Polling to check if the index is ready. This may take up to a minute.
vector_index is ready for querying.

2024/10/17 09:38:21 Creating the index.
2024/10/17 09:38:22 Polling to confirm successful index creation.
2024/10/17 09:38:22 NOTE: This may take up to a minute.
2024/10/17 09:38:48 Name of Index Created: vector_index

<dependencies>
   <!-- MongoDB Java Sync Driver v5.2.0 or later -->
   <dependency>
      <groupId>org.mongodb</groupId>
      <artifactId>mongodb-driver-sync</artifactId>
      <version>[5.2.0,)</version>
   </dependency>
</dependencies>

Successfully created a vector index named: [vector_index]
It may take up to a minute for the index to build before you can query using it.
Polling to confirm the index has completed building.
vector_index index is ready to query

from pymongo.mongo_client import MongoClient
from pymongo.operations import SearchIndexModel
import time

# Connect to your deployment
uri = "<connectionString>"
client = MongoClient(uri)

# Access your database and collection
database = client["sample_mflix"]
collection = database["embedded_movies"]

# Create your index model, then create the search index
search_index_model = SearchIndexModel(
  definition={
    "fields": [
      {
        "type": "vector",
        "path": "plot_embedding_voyage_3_large",
        "numDimensions": 2048,
        "similarity": "dotProduct",
        "quantization": "scalar"
      },
      {
        "type": "filter",
        "path": "genres"
      },
      {
        "type": "filter",
        "path": "year"
      }
    ]
  },
  name="vector_index",
  type="vectorSearch"
)

result = collection.create_search_index(model=search_index_model)
print("New search index named " + result + " is building.")

# Wait for initial sync to complete
print("Polling to check if the index is ready. This may take up to a minute.")
predicate=None
if predicate is None:
  predicate = lambda index: index.get("queryable") is True

while True:
  indices = list(collection.list_search_indexes(result))
  if len(indices) and predicate(indices[0]):
    break
  time.sleep(5)
print(result + " is ready for querying.")

client.close()

sections.heading: Run Queries Using the $vectorSearch Aggregation Pipeline Stage
Overview
sections.body:
This section demonstrates how to query the indexed vector data in
the `sample_mflix.embedded_movies` collection using the
the [$vectorSearch](pipe.$vectorSearch) stage. These sample queries also demonstrate
the various [evaluation query operators](manual/reference/mql/query-predicates) and [aggregation pipeline operators](manual/reference/mql/expressions) operators that we can use
in the query to pre-filter the data that we perform the semantic
search on.
